inbound_signal: The human confirmed that full declared-tree activation is an intended testing strength, accepted runtime missing-tag failures for this frame, and deferred static tag analysis.
decision_delta: Bumped the confirmed frame to version 2 without changing its objective, walls, thresholds, or action envelope; steered active DKRs not to treat eager graph activation or runtime tag provision as defects.
expected_effect: Keep the design native to the scope seam and prevent a speculative ownership or tag-analysis primitive from entering the current delivery.
evidence_delta: Frame v2 and tree v3 verify; the DKR-1 candidate independently observed nested runtime reuse and sibling isolation while retaining runtime-provided tags.
problem_restatement: Use one entry execution to activate the explicit graph, substitute dependencies at createScope, and add only lifecycle or observation behavior proven missing by replay.
alternatives_considered: Treat eager graph activation as an implementation defect
Require compile-time tag availability in this frame
Retain eager declared-graph activation and defer static tag analysis
assumptions_at_risk: Runtime missing-tag errors remain understandable enough until static analysis exists
Scope substitution covers practical test isolation without tree-wide mocks
Declared graph activation does not introduce hidden effect edges
dkr_decision: Continue replay and challenge of DKR-1 under frame v2; product implementation remains blocked until its cleanup counterexample is independently validated.

hallucination_sweep.claim: A runtime-required tag is itself an implicit dependency.
hallucination_sweep.verdict: rejected when the tag is a declared dependency supplied at exec

missing_context_sweep.claim: Static tag analysis is required for the 16-contract objective.
missing_context_sweep.verdict: rejected; explicitly deferred by the human

unexpected_results.claim: The first activation probe supports current-owned session reuse and exposes cleanup mutation instead of an ownership gap.
unexpected_results.verdict: candidate only; independent replay and challenge required

anti_goal_violation_review.claim: Accepting DKR-1 now would violate the independence wall and cleanup threshold.
anti_goal_violation_review.verdict: held; checkpoint remains candidate and implementation remains fenced
